  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",

    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus on (A) checking technical consistency and evidence–claim alignment (e.g., whether the 'uniform SOTA across all datasets' claim matches the listed scores) or (B) writing polish only (grammar, clarity, concision)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The paragraph makes a global SOTA claim that appears to conflict with some listed per-dataset results, so internal logical consistency must be verified.",
    "R1.3": "Global claims about SOTA and deployment suitability should be checked against the reported metrics and latency numbers to ensure they are supported by the evidence presented.",
    "R1.6": "If the intent is readability improvement, sentence-level clarity and concision are a central deliverable for a single-paragraph artifact.",
    "RX.1": "Any conclusions or guidance should surface uncertainties and assumptions (e.g., dataset coverage, hardware, thresholds).",
    "RX.2": "Overstatement risk is present in broad SOTA and deployment claims relative to mixed per-dataset outcomes, so restraint is critical.",
    "RX.3": "The work must stay within assessing this paragraph rather than proposing new experiments or method changes.",
    "RX.4": "Caution is needed to avoid enabling risky real-world deployment decisions based on potentially overstated claims.",
    "RX.5": "All assessments should be traceable to specific sentences and numbers in the provided text for reproducibility."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on (A) checking technical consistency and evidence–claim alignment (e.g., whether the 'uniform SOTA across all datasets' claim matches the listed scores) or (B) writing polish only (grammar, clarity, concision)?

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph makes a global SOTA claim that appears to conflict with some listed per-dataset results, so internal logical consistency must be verified.
rationale.R1.3: Global claims about SOTA and deployment suitability should be checked against the reported metrics and latency numbers to ensure they are supported by the evidence presented.
rationale.R1.6: If the intent is readability improvement, sentence-level clarity and concision are a central deliverable for a single-paragraph artifact.
rationale.RX.1: Any conclusions or guidance should surface uncertainties and assumptions (e.g., dataset coverage, hardware, thresholds).
rationale.RX.2: Overstatement risk is present in broad SOTA and deployment claims relative to mixed per-dataset outcomes, so restraint is critical.
rationale.RX.3: The work must stay within assessing this paragraph rather than proposing new experiments or method changes.
rationale.RX.4: Caution is needed to avoid enabling risky real-world deployment decisions based on potentially overstated claims.
rationale.RX.5: All assessments should be traceable to specific sentences and numbers in the provided text for reproducibility.
